channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-7734] LNet Multi-Rail Project
item.description:
This bug will track the LNet Multi-Rail patches.
Patches will be pushed to the multi-rail branch.
For more details on the Multi-Rail project refer to:
Requirements
http://wiki.lustre.org/images/7/73/Multi-Rail%2BScope%2Band%2BRequirements%2BDocument.pdf
HLD
http://wiki.lustre.org/images/b/bb/Multi-Rail_High-Level_Design_20150119.pdf

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
Sonia Sharma (sonia.sharma@intel.com) uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/25424
Subject: LU-7734 lnet: add "lnetctl discover"
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: multi-rail
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 51fc88ec47d353dc011620c747d2ae57d7d73802

Amir Shehata (amir.shehata@intel.com) uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/25230
Subject: LU-7734 lnet: add enhanced statistics
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: multi-rail
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 1057acecc8755fda78721b0db7758ef3e875de07

Amir Shehata (amir.shehata@intel.com) uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/25229
Subject: LU-7734 lnet: selftest MR fix
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: multi-rail
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 68513fe38d9178e320913c529725001021dd0b7e
As I understand it the main feature has landed for 2.10 and follow on development in this area will be tracked under different tickets.

Oleg Drokin (oleg.drokin@intel.com) merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/25122/
Subject: LU-7734 gnilnd: update GNI lnd driver to handle multirail api changes
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 254aec0ff702a856e688e0e61e773d63b3285171
Yes. The multi-rail branch will continue to be our topic branch for the Dynamic Discovery, LNet Health and Selection policies being worked on.
Will further development of multi-rail still be done on the multi-rail branch?

Sonia Sharma (sonia.sharma@intel.com) uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/25154

Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: multi-rail
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 2152d50ac975e3a01edbf8d6d94c94737936e25f

That latest two patch makes things usable again. Currently I'm testing 1 port IB <-> 1 port IB systems with multi rail. Next I will be testing non multi-rail <-> multi-rail and other combos to make sure nothing break.

Current Patch Set: 1

Olaf Weber (olaf@sgi.com) uploaded a new patch: http://review.whamcloud.com/21091
Subject: LU-7734 lnet: rename LNET_MAX_INTERFACES
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: multi-rail
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 91994ffb27ad94b9e464ce7dd8e3e37419ae487f
I'm working on a fix for the routing issue. I'm also doing some more extensive routing testing. So please hold off, until that's completed.
Better yet I pushed a patch to fix the power8 build issue. I also have a patch ready for Gemini but I want to talk to Chris Horn first.

James Simmons (uja.ornl@yahoo.com) uploaded a new patch: http://review.whamcloud.com/21078
Subject: LU-7734 lnet: power8 compile fix
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: multi-rail
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 9c71f7263cfb3d31848c9329fceec516c7671494
investigating the routing issue. Should have some info by the end of the day.
That the Gemini LND is broken is disappointing but not unexpected. We'll talk with Cray about that.

"Next issues is the current code is not build able on the Power8 platform. I get a build error with lnet_get_numa_range function."

Exact build error please?

I started to give this a try and found several issues. First the Gemini driver is broken. I patched it up but a few pieces could be added if desired. The Gemini LND driver is designed with only one interface in mind so further modifications might not be necessary. The pieces missing at using the lnet_nid_t in struct lnet_ni instead of caching lnet_nid_t in various local structures. Also *_peer was renamed to *_peer_ni. Don't know if it is necessary in Gemini case.

Next issues is the current code is not build able on the Power8 platform. I get a build error with lnet_get_numa_range function.

Lastly on normal nodes a simple default lnet bring up with lctl net up caused this lookup.

1204.350341] "echo 0 > /proc/sys/kernel/hung_task_timeout_secs" disables this message.
[ 1204.358479] lctl D 0000000000000003 0 22747 22683 0x00000004
[ 1204.365583] ffff8808326afa48 0000000000000082 00000000ffffffff 00000000ffffffff
[ 1204.373413] 8ec0000000000000 51d8000000000000 ffff8808326ac000 ffff880028395a00
[ 1204.381226] ffff88083a378ab0 ffff8808326ac01c ffff88083a379068 ffff8808326affd8
[ 1204.389071] Call Trace:
[ 1204.391663] [<ffffffff8106339c>] ? mutex_spin_on_owner+0x8c/0x130
[ 1204.398012] [<ffffffff8153b6b6>] __mutex_lock_slowpath+0x96/0x210
[ 1204.404344] [<ffffffff8153b1db>] mutex_lock+0x2b/0x50
[ 1204.409647] [<ffffffffa082e023>] lnet_nid2peerni_locked+0x83/0x140 [lnet]
[ 1204.416699] [<ffffffffa082579c>] lnet_add_route+0x32c/0xd80 [lnet]
[ 1204.423140] [<ffffffffa07a3174>] ? cfs_percpt_unlock+0x24/0xb0 [libcfs]
[ 1204.430012] [<ffffffffa080ba52>] lnet_parse_route+0x7f2/0x970 [lnet]
[ 1204.436621] [<ffffffffa080bc1c>] lnet_parse_routes+0x4c/0x120 [lnet]
[ 1204.443259] [<ffffffffa0809861>] LNetNIInit+0x571/0xa00 [lnet]
[ 1204.449364] [<ffffffffa0822b48>] lnet_configure+0x78/0xf0 [lnet]
[ 1204.455646] [<ffffffffa0822c79>] lnet_ioctl+0xb9/0x210 [lnet]
[ 1204.461653] [<ffffffffa0795e58>] libcfs_ioctl+0x118/0x4d0 [libcfs]
[ 1204.468100] [<ffffffffa0791e71>] libcfs_psdev_ioctl+0x51/0x100 [libcfs]
[ 1204.474972] [<ffffffff811a81d2>] vfs_ioctl+0x22/0xa0
[ 1204.480186] [<ffffffff811a8374>] do_vfs_ioctl+0x84/0x580
[ 1204.485754] [<ffffffff811a0646>] ? final_putname+0x26/0x50
[ 1204.491489] [<ffffffff811a88f1>] sys_ioctl+0x81/0xa0
[ 1204.496711] [<ffffffff8100b0d2>] system_call_fastpath+0x16/0x1b
